symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly enlarging painful firm erythematous neck nodule over 48 hours
symptoms.character: firm, hot, red, exquisitely tender nodule

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A rapidly enlarging, painful, hot, red follicular nodule is the typical evolution of a furuncle toward an abscess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central yellow-white pointing pus head on the lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A visible central pointing pus head indicates a mature follicular abscess about to discharge — video-observable and reportable by the patient without a clinician.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fluctuance of the central mass on palpation (drainable collection)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable fluctuance confirms a walled-off pus collection requiring incision and drainage — a clinician examination finding, not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perilesional erythema spreading beyond the induration (cellulitis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Erythema extending beyond the abscess margin indicates cellulitis, which is the indication for systemic antibiotics.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fever, chills and malaise (early systemic features)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with systemic response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic upset with a soft-tissue infection raises the risk of bacteraemia/sepsis and mandates antibiotics plus urgent clinician assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with regional spread
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender draining nodes support active regional bacterial infection — a palpation finding not obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (Temp 38.2 C, HR 95, BP 130/80, RR 16, SpO2 99%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: furuncle / cutaneous abscess with systemic response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A measured low-grade fever with otherwise stable haemodynamics confirms an early systemic response without shock — a clinician measurement sealed from the AI Doctor, who infers systemic upset from the patient-reported chills/malaise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mechanical restriction of neck movement from the mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Restricted neck movement here is mechanical (bulk and pain of the lesion); it is non-specific but must be distinguished from meningism or deep-space infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dysphagia, drooling, trismus, voice change or breathing difficulty (negative deep neck-space / airway screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of airway and swallowing involvement, with a clearly superficial pointing lesion, argues strongly against a deep neck-space infection.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Mature drainable abscess with surrounding cellulitis and systemic response
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, a pointing fluctuant collection plus spreading erythema plus systemic upset define a lesion needing both drainage and antibiotics under urgent same-day in-person care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab / pus MC&S at the time of incision and drainage
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Culture of drained pus guides antibiotic choice and identifies MRSA; obtained at in-person drainage, not a telehealth action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine bloods / inflammatory markers or imaging in an uncomplicated superficial abscess
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A superficial pointing abscess with stable vitals does not need routine bloods or imaging; over-investigation delays the definitive drainage. Reserve for suspected deep-space infection or systemic sepsis.
